results.identified_issue: The 'lock overlay on a particular item' is LockedPeekOverlay (a frosted ZStack with a lock icon) applied via .overlay to peek pages in QuestionIntroCard and ArgumentFeedCard. It was purely cosmetic — the actual scroll gate is the bounce-back in handleLanding/unlockedIndex, which is untouched, so scrolling past an unheard page is still prevented. The 'scroll bar' the reporter means is PlaybackIndicator (a read-only playback position bar), not an actual scroll control. I left the transient 'Finish listening to this one first' toast at the top of the feed in place since it explains the bounce; if that should go too, say so. Kept the now-unused accent param on PlaybackIndicator to keep the call sites stable in case accent coloring returns.
results.summary: Removed the per-item lock overlay and made the playback bar full-width and gray
results.detailed_description: Three changes, all in the Discover feed. 1) Removed the lock overlay that frosted a not-yet-earned page and stamped a lock icon on it. That was just chrome — the feed already bounces you back when you try to scroll onto a page you haven't heard, and that scroll gate is unchanged, so you still can't skip ahead. The page beyond just no longer wears the frosted lock. 2) The playback position bar now runs the full width of the phone instead of sitting inset with margins on both sides. It's a read-only indicator (not a real scrubber — you can't drag it), so spanning the screen reads cleaner. The time labels underneath keep their margins. 3) Recolored that bar from the side's accent color to a subtle gray so it recedes against the video instead of drawing the eye. I couldn't run the app to screenshot it (build/deploy happens after handoff), so this is verified by reading the layout, not by eye — flag anything that looks off and I'll adjust.

description: No need to have the lock screen, the lock overlay on a particular item. Just preventing the scroll is sufficient. It kind of looks bad at the bottom of the screen now. Also let's extend the width of the scroll bar to be the full width of the phone since we're not actually using it anymore. And then let's also make it like a kind of a gray color so it's a bit more subtle.
